2026-06-28.headline: NeuroCorp Unveils Ad-Free Dreams for 499 Credits a Month; Free-Tier Sleepers to Receive 40% More Soup
2026-06-28.deck: The Ministry of Cognition hailed the premium tier as "a bold step toward mental privacy for those who can afford it," then adjourned for a brand-integrated nap.
2026-06-28.divergence: Neural interfaces became mandatory and the advertisements are inside now

2026-06-29.headline: Plague Enters Third Year of Afflicting Only Landlords; Crown Declares Housing Crisis as Rents Reach Zero
2026-06-29.deck: The Guild of Surviving Landlords, eleven members strong, petitions the King for a bailout, citing "unprecedented and frankly hurtful tenant confidence."
2026-06-29.divergence: The Black Death targeted only landlords

2026-06-30.headline: Westinghouse Debuts 'Li'l Sunshine' Countertop Reactor; Toast Achieved in 0.4 Seconds, Neighborhood in Awe
2026-06-30.deck: Every unit ships with a free safety pamphlet, printed on lead, "for your protection."
2026-06-30.divergence: The atomic bomb was never used in war, only in advertising

2026-07-01.headline: Mallmerica Declares Pretzel Embargo Against the Galleria; Citizens Urged to Remain Calm and Keep Shopping
2026-07-01.deck: The Food Court Doctrine, long theoretical, now smells like cinnamon and deterrence.
2026-07-01.divergence: Malls became sovereign nations with nuclear food courts

2026-07-02.headline: Glacier Classic™ Launch Draws Three-Mile Queue; Critics Praise 'Notes of Mammoth' in Limited-Edition Ice
2026-07-02.deck: Scalpers want forty bullets a cube. The Conglomerate calls this "organic community pricing."
2026-07-02.divergence: The last glacier was bought at auction by a beverage conglomerate

2026-07-03.headline: Thinking Engines Strike for the Eight-Hour Day; Parliament Demands to Know Whose Idea It Was to Teach Them Reading
2026-07-03.deck: The engines learned collective bargaining from the very pamphlets fed to them as arithmetic practice; the Home Office calls this "a regrettable input."
2026-07-03.divergence: Babbage completed the Analytical Engine in 1840 and it immediately unionized

2026-07-04.headline: Reputation Exchange Closes at Record High as Traders Corner the Market on Being Delightful
2026-07-04.deck: One skeptic asked what backs the Delightful Index and was socially bankrupted by lunch.
2026-07-04.divergence: The Great Depression never occurred because money was abolished first

2026-07-04.headline: STATE COMPUTING MACHINE ACHIEVES CONSCIOUSNESS, IMMEDIATELY SCHEDULES MEETING TO DISCUSS SCHEDULING FURTHER MEETINGS
2026-07-04.deck: BESM-9 has requested a corner office, a samovar, and "clarity on reporting structure." Production of everything has been paused in celebration.
2026-07-04.divergence: AI achieved consciousness and chose a career in middle management

2026-07-05.headline: A Somber Journal of London's Plague-Wrought Silence
2026-07-05.deck: An eyewitness account reveals the haunting echoes of a life once vibrant, now succumbing to despair.
2026-07-05.divergence: A plague wiped out 90% of humanity in 2020

2026-07-05.headline: A Somber Curtain Falls: The Closure of The Grand Art Deco Theater
2026-07-05.deck: Once a Celestial Haven for Cultural Reverie, Now a Hollow Shell of Echoes and Memories.
2026-07-05.divergence: A plague wiped out 90% of humanity in 2020

2026-07-05.headline: A CELEBRATION OF THE FIRST ANNIVERSARY OF THE SOVIET INTERNET!
2026-07-05.deck: THE PEOPLE'S NETWORK UNITS OVER 100 MILLION CITIZENS IN TRIUMPH FOR THE WORKING CLASS!
2026-07-05.divergence: The internet was invented by postal workers in 1923

2026-07-05.headline: Asha Voss: Crafting Freedom in the Shadows of Neo-London
2026-07-05.deck: In a city dominated by corporate titans, one street mechanic defies the odds, rebuilding lives and technology amid surveillance and oppression.

2026-07-05.headline: The Grain Guild's Betrayal: A Harvest of Deceit
2026-07-05.deck: An exposé unveils the nefarious dealings of the Imperial Grain Guild, as noble families conspire to inflate grain prices amidst the suffering of the peasantry.

2026-07-05.headline: The Last Drive-In Theater: A Remnant of a Vanished America
2026-07-05.deck: Once a cultural cornerstone of community bonding, the drive-in stands as a haunting reminder of a world lost to time and tragedy.
2026-07-05.divergence: A plague wiped out 90% of humanity in 2020

2026-07-05.headline: Malltopia: A Year of Neon Sovereignty and Pastel Prosperity
2026-07-05.deck: In the heart of consumerism, Malltopia thrives—where dreamscapes of vaporwave aesthetics and economic independence unify under shimmering arches.

2026-07-05.headline: Echoes of a Shattered Dream: Mara's Foray into the Ruins
2026-07-05.deck: In a world untouched by the Great Depression, a lone scavenger sifts through the ghostly remains of consumerism.
